features.name: Harness context budget pass
features.description: Reduce startup token load by keeping root state files concise and moving detailed evidence to reports and version docs.
definition_of_done: AGENTS.md tells future agents to read root state first and avoid full changelog/report ingestion unless needed.
feature_list.json tracks only the current feature, one near-term art track, and recent harness maintenance.
progress.md gives restart state, blockers, verification evidence, and next step in under about 1,000 words.
Root and docs session handoffs point to detailed reports instead of repeating every implementation event.
harness-creator validation stays green.

features.next_step: Keep detailed implementation history in reports and update root files only with summaries.

npm run harness:gate passes after any fixes.

features.name: Core fun polish (first 30 minute UX)
features.description: Polish the first 30 minute experience after the v0.56 slice locked: launch impact connoting card cause, card effect visibility, balanced product catalogue, staff burnout pacing, and year-two onboarding flow.
definition_of_done: First launch result has entry animations and shine.
Cards explicitly visualize their effect (card name -> arrow -> effects) at launch impact and reward choice.
Curated 30 product catalogue covers education, mobility, robotics, semiconductors, and odd industries.
Burnout aftermath progress/quality penalty is eased so early game does not become fatigue avoidance.
First 30 minute attrition risks (first screen impact, first hire pulse, year-two onboarding ambiguity, release ETA legibility, reward rarity differentiation) are reduced.

features.name: Market season strength (rival presence, market share visualization, response cards, big event popup)

Closed 2026-05-29 across 5 isolated blocks (#1 visualization, #2 history+sparkline, #3 archetype/weakness, #4 response card differentiation, #5 big event popup) plus a closeout commit. All blocks were derive-only or queue-only — no simulation tick behavior changes.

Top pressure competitor highlighted with shared color/badge.
Rival archetype/weakness surfaced near the market share panel.
Response card differentiation makes rival pressure feel like a card need.

features.next_step: Closed. Next milestone selected as v0.60-alpha-boundaryless-industry-expansion on 2026-05-29 (3 new physical industries + cross-industry synergies, decomposed into blocks #1-#4).

features.name: Boundaryless industry expansion slice (3 new physical industries + cross-industry synergies + combos)
features.description: Validate the fun of expanding beyond software into physical industries (ROADMAP v0.60, renumbered from v0.59 after resource-visibility took the v0.59 number). Add 3 new physical-industry domains (manufacturing, logistics, energy) on top of the existing 12, wire robot/manufacturing/logistics requirement conditions, add 10 cross-industry synergies and 10 high-risk/high-reward combos. Controlled slice only — ROADMAP §5 forbids mass industry expansion, so this stays at exactly 3 new industries. Multi-session; decomposed into isolated blocks.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (DONE 2026-05-29): 3 new physical-industry domains (manufacturing, logistics, energy) added to data/domains.json with unlock_requirements reusing existing capabilities; 6 starter products (2 per domain) gated so none are launchable at game start; new domains flow into the existing boundaryless-expansion goals + product domain filters; ?scenario=physical-industries added; tests for locked filters / QA route / boundaryless goals; harness:gate 43 files / 420 tests; simulation.ts untouched.

Block #2 (DONE 2026-05-29): added manufacturing + logistics capabilities (completing the robot/manufacturing/logistics trio with the existing robotics); manufacturing unlocks_domains {1:manufacturing,3:energy} effects automation, logistics unlocks_domains {1:logistics} effects automation/data; re-gated the 3 physical domains to the trio; data-driven so simulation.ts untouched; harness:gate 43 files / 421 tests.

Block #3 (DONE 2026-05-29): 10 cross-industry synergies in data/industry_synergies.json (each pairs 2 domains, physical-industry focused) + new src/game/industry-synergies.ts getIndustrySynergySummary mirroring the office synergy pattern; monthly_effects hooked into getMonthlyStrategicEffects via a minimal 2-line aggregation alongside office/deck synergies; validate-data rules + UI surfacing + tests; harness:gate 43 files / 425 tests.

Block #4 (DONE 2026-05-29): 10 high-risk/high-reward combos in data/industry_combos.json (2-3 high-tier physical domains each, big rewards cash 430-900 / users 260-620 plus a real negative downside e.g. trust/compute/talent + risk_label) via new src/game/industry-combos.ts cloned from industry-synergies; minimal 2-line hook in getMonthlyStrategicEffects; deterministic (no RNG); product_ideas.compatibility_rules untouched (§5); harness:gate 43 files / 428 tests.
Each block adds at least one test; npm run harness:gate passes; no unintended simulation regressions.
Stays a controlled 3-industry slice per ROADMAP §5 (no mass expansion).

features.next_step: Closed. Next milestone is v0.61-alpha public web alpha candidate (save/load stability, 10-year campaign completable, tutorial flow, no UI breaks, intro copy/screenshots). Before beta, refresh reports/v0_60_status_and_promo.html (user request 2026-05-29).

features.name: Public web alpha candidate (stabilization: save/load, 10-year completability, tutorial, UI, intro materials)
features.description: Stabilize the accumulated v0.56-v0.60 build into a publicly testable web alpha. No new large systems (ROADMAP §5) — harden save/load migration, prove the 10-year campaign completes end-to-end, clean up the tutorial flow, remove major UI breaks, and prepare intro copy/screenshots. Investigation 2026-05-29: save/load is already mature (SAVE_VERSION 11, serializeGameState/hydrateGameState with per-field sanitizers + createRecoveryState + validateGameStateIntegrity + save-integrity.test.ts; App.tsx localStorage auto-save + offline settlement) and run-simulator.ts already simulates the 10-year campaign with alpha-readiness gates. So this milestone hardens, it does not build from scratch. Multi-session; decomposed into blocks.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (DONE 2026-05-29): save/load migration hardening. +257 lines of tests in save-integrity.test.ts (full late-game round-trip across v0.58-v0.60 fields, legacy-save missing-field migration, malformed manufacturing/logistics capability hydration, offline settlement on a late-game state). hydrateGameState audit found a capability raw-merge gap and fixed it with a new sanitizeCapabilityMap (clamps each canonical capability to [0,max_level], defaults missing). SAVE_VERSION kept at 11 (serialized shape unchanged; migration is initialState-merge based; version-8 legacy load tested). No simulation tick changes. harness:gate 43 files / 431 tests.

Block #2 (DONE 2026-05-29): all 3 growth-path strategies complete to month 120 (success, integrity ok, finale, 10 annual reviews) — proven by new run-simulator.test.ts cases. evaluateAlphaReadiness strengthened to require ALL strategies complete (was productivity_line only); code_vision_lab auto-play now expands into v0.60 physical industries (manufacturing/logistics/energy) via real game checks; trust_enterprise gets a portfolio fallback. Stale versionTarget labels refreshed to v0.61-alpha. No softlock found; simulation.ts tick untouched (changes confined to run-simulator.ts harness policy). harness:gate 43 files / 433 tests.

Block #3 (DONE 2026-05-29): tutorial flow cleanup + mobile UI contract. Reordered the 8-step tutorial so product_ideas follows the first launch reward (was appearing too early) and locked invariants (valid targetMenu, unique ids, reachability, core-loop order) via tutorial-guide.test.ts; added mobile 390x844 layout-contract coverage for the v0.60 physical-industries panel + industry synergy/combo UI (single-column <=520px, min-width:0 + text-wrap, no horizontal overflow) with a minimal App.css fix. simulation.ts + save path untouched. Pixel/screenshot QA left as a manual follow-up (Playwright unavailable). harness:gate 43 files / 437 tests.

Block #4 (DONE 2026-05-29): refreshed reports/v0_60_status_and_promo.html to v0.61 (version, 437 tests, v0.60-done/v0.61-in-progress roadmap, tightened release prediction ~2-3.5 months, stabilization story); wrote reports/v0_61_public_alpha_intro.md (KO/EN intro copy, taglines, how-to-start, highlights, screenshot shot-list). Claude Code direct (docs, not code). Screenshots left as a manual capture follow-up (Playwright unavailable).
Each block adds tests where applicable; npm run harness:gate passes; no simulation tick regressions.

features.name: Payoff juice (dopamine polish: combo/synergy activation celebration, discovery moments, milestone fanfare)
features.description: Pre-beta dopamine polish slice (like v0.57 core-fun-polish). Amplifies EXISTING moments — industry combo/synergy activation, milestones, annual-review near-miss — into payoff celebrations. §5-safe: no new game systems, only amplifies what already fires. Sets up the dopamine layer ahead of the post-1.0 roguelike run-modifier + tag-derivation vision (see reports/v0_62_design_direction.md). User direction 2026-05-29: dopamine = discovery x variety x payoff-juice x tension; payoff-juice is the immediate win, tension/relief is partly a later difficulty-tuning concern.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (DONE 2026-05-29): high-risk combo / top synergy ACTIVATION celebration. New pure-derive helper src/game/payoff-activation.ts (getPayoffCelebrationMoments maps active combos/synergies to named moments; getNewPayoffActivationIds = set-diff) — NO GameState field, the 'previously active' set lives in React state, so the save path is untouched. PayoffCelebrationModal.tsx mirrors BigEventModal (queue + dismiss + effect summary + glow/flare + prefers-reduced-motion). ?scenario=payoff-juice demo. simulation.ts untouched. harness:gate 44 files / 439 tests (a new unit-test file shifts the long-standing 43-file baseline to 44).

Block #2 (DONE 2026-05-29): discovery moments + collection-lite. New persisted GameState.discoveredPayoffIds (mirrors seenTutorials: createInitialState [] + sanitizeStringArray hydrate, old-save migration to [], round-trip tested); first activation marks discovered + shows a '신규 발견!' celebration variant; a collection view (discovered=revealed, undiscovered=???, N/total progress) + ?scenario=collection. simulation.ts only got the initial-state default + hydrate sanitizer (no tick/balance). harness:gate 44 files / 444 tests.

Block #3 (DONE 2026-05-29): milestone fanfare + annual-review near-miss. Reused the existing achievements system — fanfare on unlockedAchievements growth (UI set-diff, conditions/timing unchanged) + 2 conservative whitelist-safe achievements (first_major_war_chest min_cash 1M reward cash 15k; agi_threshold_lab min_capability_upgrades 18 reward compute 80/data 60). New derive-only getAnnualReviewNearMissSignal (margin from annualReviewHistory, pass criteria unchanged) emphasizes barely-passed/recovered reviews. ?scenario=milestones. simulation.ts/types.ts/save schema untouched. harness:gate 44 files / 448 tests.

Each block adds tests; npm run harness:gate passes; §5-safe (amplify existing moments, no new systems); prefers-reduced-motion + mobile 390x844 respected; no unintended simulation tick/balance changes.
evidence: 2026-05-29: Selected after v0.61 + the design-direction review. User picked the dopamine direction (tag-derivation engine A + multi-ending/roguelike B + art-first/parallel C) and asked for payoff-juice as the immediate, §5-safe win. Block #1 (combo/synergy activation celebration) delegated to Codex CLI. The big systems (roguelike run modifiers, tag-derivation engine, multi-ending) are recorded as post-1.0 in reports/v0_62_design_direction.md.

2026-05-29: Block #1 implemented by Codex CLI (fast/medium) and verified by Claude Code. Pure-derive celebration (payoff-activation.ts) + PayoffCelebrationModal + ?scenario=payoff-juice; no GameState field, simulation.ts + save path untouched (zero balance/tick risk). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 44 files / 439 tests.

2026-05-29: Block #2 implemented by Codex CLI (fast/medium) and verified by Claude Code. Persisted GameState.discoveredPayoffIds (seenTutorials pattern) with save round-trip + old-save migration tested; discovery '신규 발견!' variant + collection-lite view + ?scenario=collection. simulation.ts diff = initialState default + hydrate sanitizer only (no tick/balance); discovery marking is a UI-driven setGameState update. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 44 files / 444 tests.

2026-05-29: Block #3 implemented by Codex CLI (fast/medium, re-run after a killed first attempt was reverted clean) and verified by Claude Code. Milestone fanfare reuses achievements (unlockedAchievements set-diff) + 2 conservative achievements; getAnnualReviewNearMissSignal derive (pass criteria unchanged). simulation.ts/types.ts/save schema untouched. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 44 files / 448 tests.

2026-05-29: v0.62 CLOSED. All 3 dopamine-polish blocks shipped (29c2dc9 activation celebration, a115131 discovery+collection, this commit milestone+near-miss). §5-safe throughout — only block #2 added a persisted field (discoveredPayoffIds, save-migrated) and only block #3 added 2 conservative achievements; no simulation tick behavior change. Tests 437 -> 448. Sets up the dopamine layer ahead of the post-1.0 roguelike + tag-engine vision.
features.next_step: Closed. Next is beta prep (real-human playtest + final art, parallel; art resolution up) toward v1.0, or start a post-1.0 big system from reports/v0_62_design_direction.md (tag-derivation engine, roguelike run modifiers, multi-ending).

features.name: Roguelike run modifiers (city x world-lore x market x founder + yearly events)
features.description: First post-1.0 big system, started early by explicit user decision 2026-05-29 (option 1). This LIFTS the ROADMAP §5 'no new large systems' hold for THIS system only — it is the controlled replayability slice, exactly as v0.60's industry slice was a controlled exception to '산업군 대량 확장'. A run is seeded from start city (서울/도쿄/뉴욕/SF/텍사스...), world-lore (e.g. 알파고 정체 / 비트코인 GPU 초고가 / 오픈소스 천국 / 규제 강국), market condition, and founder trait; yearly world-events keep mutating the run. Goal: replayability — '한 판 더'. Grounded on existing company_locations (modifier shape), campaign_shocks (yearly events), and roguelite meta state. Multi-session; decomposed into blocks. Design: reports/v0_62_design_direction.md §3.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (DONE 2026-05-29): run-modifier FOUNDATION. data/run_modifiers.json (global start_cities incl. tokyo/seoul/etc, world_lore incl. standard + GPU-expensive/research-slow/etc, market_conditions, founder_traits — starting deltas + tags). New pure module run-modifiers.ts (selectRunModifierConfig / applyRunModifierStartingDeltas / sanitizeRunModifiersState); createInitialState(selection?) + resetRunWithMetaUnlocks integrate it; GameState.runModifiers field save-migrated (old saves + default config = standard no-op). Monthly tick UNTOUCHED; default config regression-tested = current initial state. ?scenario=run-modifiers (Tokyo + GPU-expensive + enterprise-winter + researcher). harness:gate 45 files / 454 tests.

Block #2 (DONE 2026-05-29): world-lore/market tick effects. getRunModifierMonthlyEffects(state) (run-modifiers.ts) sums conservative monthly ResourceMaps per active tag (run_modifiers.json tag_effects — compute_expensive compute -8, market_boom cash/users +100, enterprise_winter cash -80, etc.), hooked into getMonthlyStrategicEffects via the SAME minimal 2-line pattern as v0.60 synergies/combos. Standard world (tags []) is a verified no-op (regression guard: calculateMonthlyEconomy unchanged). Additive only (no cost multipliers); deterministic. harness:gate 45 files / 457 tests.

Block #3 (DONE 2026-05-29): yearly world-events — a PARALLEL layer mirroring campaign_shocks (campaign_shocks untouched). data/world_events.json pool + WorldEventDefinition type; deterministic seed-based schedule (runModifiers.seed + worldLoreId, no RNG) decides which events fire which year; applyDueWorldEvents(state) dedupes via new GameState.worldEventHistory (save-migrated, old saves -> []) and applies conservative additive resource_effects; ONE-line advanceMonth hook next to applyDueCampaignShocks. ?scenario=world-events. harness:gate 46 files / 464 tests.

Block #4 (DONE 2026-05-30): 세계 뽑기 roll + reveal UI — the payoff that makes the system engage in real play. New pure rollRunModifierSelection(seed) in run-modifiers.ts; the new-run UI callsites (resetRunWithMetaUnlocks in GameChrome handleStartNextRun + MenuPanels quick-start/starter-deck/meta-unlock buttons) pass a freshly-rolled seed so post-onboarding runs start in a non-standard world; first onboarding run (createInitialState() no-arg) + every no-arg reset stay standard (regression-tested). New WorldRevealModal.tsx (mirrors PayoffCelebrationModal) reveals the rolled city x world x market x founder from state.runModifiers with sequential dopamine + seed shown, once-per-run via ephemeral React state. ?scenario=world-reveal. NO new GameState field; simulation.ts + types.ts diffs empty (tick/save untouched). harness:gate 46 files / 466 tests.
Each block adds tests; npm run harness:gate passes; save round-trip preserved; default run = current behavior (no regression); balance changes deliberate + documented.
evidence: 2026-05-29: Started by user decision (option 1, roguelike run modifiers first; §5 hold lifted for this system). Investigation: company_locations.json is a Korea-centric in-run office ladder, so global start-cities are a NEW run-modifier layer reusing the cost/talent/ai-bonus delta shape; campaign_shocks.json is the block #3 yearly-event base; resetRunWithMetaUnlocks (meta-progression.ts:119) + createInitialState (App.tsx) are the run-start integration points; GameState.discoveredPayoffIds is the field-placement + save-sanitizer pattern reference. Block #1 (foundation) delegated to Codex CLI xhigh per reports/codex-handoff/v0_63_block1_run_modifier_foundation.md.

2026-05-29: Block #1 implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. run-modifiers.ts isolates the logic (pure); createInitialState gained an optional selection param (default -> standard zero-delta = no regression, regression-tested) + resetRunWithMetaUnlocks passes it through; GameState.runModifiers {seed,startCityId,worldLoreId,marketConditionId,founderTraitId,tags} with sanitizeRunModifiersState hydrate (old saves -> standard). Monthly tick/cost untouched (empty in the simulation.ts diff). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 45 files / 454 tests.

2026-05-29: Block #2 implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. getRunModifierMonthlyEffects + a 2-line getMonthlyStrategicEffects hook (identical to v0.60 synergies); conservative tag_effects in run_modifiers.json; standard-world regression guard confirms no-op. The run-modifier system is now functional end-to-end (worlds affect the monthly economy). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 45 files / 457 tests.

2026-05-29: Block #3 implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. Yearly world-events as a parallel layer mirroring campaign_shocks EXACTLY (campaign-shocks.ts untouched, confirmed absent from the diff). New data/world_events.json (10-event pool, conservative additive resource_effects, year_range + optional world_lore_tags) + WorldEventDefinition; new world-events.ts with a deterministic seed-derived schedule (no RNG — same seed+world = same sequence) and applyDueWorldEvents dedup via GameState.worldEventHistory; simulation.ts diff is exactly the one applyDueWorldEvents line after applyDueCampaignShocks + the worldEventHistory initial-state default + hydrate sanitizer; state-integrity validates the new history; old saves migrate to []. world-events.test.ts proves seed determinism, no-double-apply, save round-trip + legacy migration, AND the standard productivity_line 10-year run still reaches month 120 non-failure with events recorded. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 46 files / 464 tests.

2026-05-30: Block #4 implemented by Codex CLI (medium/fast) and verified by Claude Code; v0.63 CLOSED. The roll+reveal payoff makes the run-modifier system actually engage in normal play (previously only QA scenarios could enter a non-standard world). New pure rollRunModifierSelection(seed); new-run UI callsites pass a rolled seed while the first onboarding run + all no-arg resets stay standard (regression-tested, so the run-simulator 10-year gate is unaffected). New WorldRevealModal mirrors PayoffCelebrationModal. simulation.ts + types.ts diffs are EMPTY (no tick/save/field change — reveal derives from state.runModifiers, once-per-run via ephemeral React state). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 46 files / 466 tests. The milestone shipped across #1 foundation / #2 world tick effects / #3 yearly events / #4 roll+reveal; tests grew 448 -> 466; the only simulation.ts touches were the additive run-modifier monthly-effect hook (#2, 2 lines) and the one applyDueWorldEvents line (#3).
features.next_step: v0.63 CLOSED (46 files / 466 tests). Next: v0.64 content-depth wave — #1 (run-modifier 4-axis expansion, ran in an isolated worktree parallel to #4, handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_64_content_wave.md) then #2 (world-events 12->26 themed to the new worlds, handoff v0_64_block2_world_events_expansion.md). Then v0.65 difficulty/challenge axis, v0.66 tag-derivation engine (decision A), v0.67 multi-ending (decision B). Master plan: reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md.

features.name: Content depth wave (roguelike variety volume)
features.description: Fill the replayability value chain's variety lever now that the v0.63 run-modifier system spine is complete (foundation + world tick effects + yearly events + roll/reveal). The run-modifier engine is PURE DATA-DRIVEN, so content scales with zero code change — the cheapest, highest-volume work and the user's north star ('풍성한 컨텐츠 · 무궁무진한 조합'). Decomposed into data-only blocks (fast/medium). Master plan: reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md. §5 stance: data-only is the safest expansion; default entries stay no-op so the standard 10-year run is unaffected.
definition_of_done: Block #1: run-modifier 4-axis content expansion — world_lore 5->12, start_cities 6->11, market_conditions 4->8, founder_traits 5->9 (~600 -> ~9,500 combos), plus tag_effects for every new monthly-acting tag. Data-only (run_modifiers.json + validate-data.mjs + new run-modifiers-content.test.ts); run-modifiers.ts/simulation.ts/types.ts/save untouched; default entries stay zero-delta no-ops (regression guard). Handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_64_content_wave.md.

Block #2: yearly world-events content expansion — world_events.json 12->26, each new event themed via world_lore_tags to a new world so worlds get a distinct event stream (selector bias already supports this, no code change). Update the block #3 pool-size test bound. Handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_64_block2_world_events_expansion.md.
Each block: harness:gate passes; standard 10-year run still completes; deterministic; data-only (no tick/save change); default run = current behavior.
evidence: 2026-05-30: Opened at v0.63 closeout. Grounded on the confirmed data-driven schema — selectOption auto-includes any new JSON entry in the seeded pool and getRunModifierMonthlyEffects sums tag_effects, so 4-axis + event content needs no code. Block #1 launched on Codex CLI (medium) in an isolated git worktree (/Users/taewookkim/dev/act-content-wave, branch v0.64-content-wave, node_modules symlinked) to run truly parallel with v0.63 #4; disjoint file ownership makes the merge conflict-free.

2026-05-30: Block #1 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (medium) in the parallel worktree, verified + landed into main by Claude Code. world_lore 5->12, start_cities 6->11, market_conditions 4->8, founder_traits 5->9 = 9,504 combos (was 600), plus tag_effects for every new monthly-acting tag. Worktree diff was data-only (run_modifiers.json + validate-data.mjs; run-modifiers.ts/simulation.ts/types.ts UNTOUCHED — confirmed). Default entries stay zero-delta no-ops (regression-guarded in the new run-modifiers-content.test.ts). validate-data.mjs run_modifiers checks extended. Files copied into main (disjoint from #4) and the union gate re-verified by Claude Code.

2026-05-30: Block #2 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (medium), verified by Claude Code; v0.64 CLOSED. world_events.json 12 -> 26 (14 new events, 2 per new world: agi_overhang/data_drought/chip_war/privacy_fortress/bigtech_monopoly/ai_winter_redux/robotics_boom), each themed via world_lore_tags so the v0.63 #3 selector biases them into that world's run (no selector code change). world-events.test.ts pool-size bound raised + a chip_war theming-bias test added; validate-data.mjs world_events bound updated. DATA-ONLY confirmed — world-events.ts/simulation.ts/types.ts/run_modifiers.json/run-modifiers.ts diffs all empty. Standard 10-year run still completes. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 47 files / 472 tests. (Note: the first launch hung on a missing stdin redirect — killed clean with no partial files, relaunched with < /dev/null.)
features.next_step: v0.64 CLOSED (47 files / 472 tests). Next: v0.65 difficulty/challenge axis — #1 foundation (challenge tier field + tier-scaled monthly headwind, standard tier no-op; handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_65_block1_difficulty_foundation.md, xhigh) then #2 reward scaling + tier-select UI. Then v0.66 tag-derivation engine (decision A), v0.67 multi-ending (decision B). Master plan reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md.

features.name: Difficulty / challenge tier axis (tension-relief + open challenge)
features.description: The user's tension-relief lever ('긴장.해소 부분은 난이도 조정 같은걸로') and the 'open challenge' (열린 도전) part of the north star. A run takes a player-chosen challenge tier (story / standard / hard / brutal); harder tiers add a tier-scaled monthly HEADWIND (additive negative ResourceMap via the same getMonthlyStrategicEffects hook pattern as v0.60/v0.63 #2 — NOT a sign-scaling of run-modifier tag_effects, which would make boom worlds easier), and clearing a harder tier multiplies the meta-progression reward. Standard tier = zero headwind = current behavior (regression-safe). Deterministic; §5-aligned (additive, no tick rewrite). Master plan reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (foundation): data/difficulty_tiers.json (story/standard/hard/brutal — monthly_headwind ResourceMap + reward_multiplier); RunModifiersState.challengeTier (save-migrated, old saves + default = standard); getDifficultyMonthlyEffects + a 2-line getMonthlyStrategicEffects hook (standard = no-op regression guard); standard 10-year completability unchanged + hard tier still completable (run-simulator). xhigh. Handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_65_block1_difficulty_foundation.md.
Block #2: clear-tier reward multiplier into meta-progression unlock points + run-setup tier selection UI + reveal shows the chosen tier. medium.
Each block: harness:gate passes; standard tier = current behavior; deterministic; balance changes deliberate + documented.
evidence: 2026-05-30: Opened at v0.64 closeout. Design chose additive tier-headwind over scaling run-modifier tag_effects (which mix signs, so scaling would make boom worlds easier — not 'harder'). Player-chosen tiers fit the 'open challenge' framing (take on a brutal world for bigger meta reward). Block #1 handoff written; xhigh because it adds a save-migrated RunModifiersState field + a monthly tick hook + balance.

2026-05-30: Block #1 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. data/difficulty_tiers.json (story/standard/hard/brutal; standard monthly_headwind {} = no-op, hard {cash:-60,hype:-1}); RunModifiersState.challengeTier added; sanitizeRunModifiersState maps missing/unknown tier -> standard (old-save migration, seenTutorials pattern); getDifficultyMonthlyEffects + a 2-line additive getMonthlyStrategicEffects hook (identical to the run-modifier hook). simulation.ts diff is exactly that hook (no other tick change); difficulty is additive headwind, NOT a sign-scale of tag_effects. run-simulator.runTenYearCampaignSimulation gained an optional runModifierSelection arg (default no-arg = standard, so the existing gate is unchanged) + a hard-tier productivity_line completability test (month 120+, non-failure, ending, integrity ok). Standard-tier no-op regression-guarded. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 47 files / 477 tests.

2026-05-30: Block #2 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (medium) and verified by Claude Code; v0.65 CLOSED. getRunInsightReward(state) now multiplies the base founder-insight reward by the run tier reward_multiplier (Math.round) — applied centrally so resetRunWithMetaUnlocks/getNextRunSetupPlan/runHistory all reflect it; standard tier x1.0 keeps the existing reward (regression test: 13 unchanged; hard 13*1.5 -> 20). Run-setup adds a tier selector that passes challengeTierId into the resetRunWithMetaUnlocks runModifierSelection (quick-start/starter-deck/meta-unlock paths); GameChrome quick new-run passes standard explicitly. WorldRevealModal shows the chosen tier + reward multiplier. ?scenario=difficulty-reward. NO new GameState field (challengeTier already in runModifiers); simulation.ts/types.ts/save schema/data JSON diffs all empty. Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 47 files / 481 tests.
features.next_step: v0.65 CLOSED (47 files / 481 tests). The roguelike track has shipped: v0.63 system spine + v0.64 content depth + v0.65 difficulty. Next per roadmap is v0.66 tag-derivation engine (decision A — the '무궁무진한 조합' centerpiece, a large multi-block system) — CHECKPOINT with the user on direction before launching (could also prioritize v0.67 multi-ending or beta prep). Master plan reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md.

features.name: Tag-derivation engine (emergent combo discovery — decision A)
features.description: The '무궁무진한 조합' centerpiece (user decision A). Today tags are a static map that just sums monthly effects. The derivation engine turns tag COMBINATIONS into emergent, DISCOVERED results — when a run accumulates a qualifying set of tags (run-modifier axes + owned industry/product tags), a hidden product/event/bonus is 'discovered' (reusing the v0.62 discoveredPayoffIds + PayoffCelebration discovery pattern). This makes the combinatorial space (9,504 start combos x derivation rules) feel effectively infinite without hand-authoring each outcome. Deterministic (seed/state-derived, no RNG). Large — decomposed into blocks; starts derive-only then expands. Master plan reports/v0_63_plus_content_roadmap.md §v0.66.
definition_of_done: Block #1 (foundation, DERIVE-ONLY): data/derivation_rules.json (~12 archetype rules — requires:[run-modifier tags] -> yields metadata + discovery_id + Korean title/description) + a pure getDerivedArchetypes(state) evaluator in new tag-derivation.ts + DerivationRuleDefinition type. No GameState field, no tick/save change, no effect application yet. ?scenario=tag-derivation + tests (matching/determinism/tag-vocab validity/spam guard). xhigh. Handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_66_block1_tag_derivation_engine.md.

Block #2: discovery celebration + persistence — reuse v0.62 PayoffCelebrationModal + the discoveredPayoffIds pattern (new or extended persisted id list) so a newly-derived archetype fires a '발견!' moment + collection entry.
Block #3: emergent effects — wire rule yields into real bonuses/events/products via an additive hook (§5-style), deterministic.
Block #4: balance — check effect magnitudes vs 10-year completability + difficulty tiers.
Each block: harness:gate passes; deterministic; derive-only or additive first; standard run unaffected unless a balance change is deliberate + documented.
evidence: 2026-05-30: User confirmed decision A (tag-derivation engine) at the post-v0.65 checkpoint over multi-ending / beta prep. Decomposed into 4 blocks (engine -> discovery -> effects -> balance). Block #1 (derive-only pure engine, grounded on the real run-modifier tag vocabulary) handed off (reports/codex-handoff/v0_66_block1_tag_derivation_engine.md) and launched on Codex CLI xhigh.

2026-05-30: Block #1 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. data/derivation_rules.json (~12 archetype rules, requires:[run-modifier tags] -> yields metadata + discovery_id + Korean title/desc) + DerivationRuleDefinition type + new pure tag-derivation.ts (getDerivedArchetypes(state):DerivationRuleDefinition[] returning rules whose requires all match state.runModifiers.tags, sorted by id; runHasTags helper). ?scenario=tag-derivation + tests (count/id-uniqueness/tag-vocab validity typo guard/known-combo match/determinism/spam guard). DERIVE-ONLY proven: git diff --exit-code on simulation.ts/App.tsx/state-integrity.ts/save-integrity.test.ts returned empty — no GameState field, no tick, no save, no effect application (yields is metadata only). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 48 files / 488 tests.

2026-05-30: Block #2 DONE — implemented by Codex CLI (xhigh) and verified by Claude Code. Cross-run archetype collection: new RogueliteState.discoveredArchetypeIds (META, not per-run); createInitialRogueliteState (deckbuilding.ts) defaults [] + accepts it; resetRunWithMetaUnlocks derives the next run's archetypes, computes new via getNewlyDiscoveredArchetypes (mirrors payoff-activation), and stores uniqueStrings([...prev, ...new]) while preserving founderInsight/unlockedMetaIds/starterDeckId/runHistory; hydrateRogueliteState sanitizes the new field against derivation rule ids (old saves -> []); state-integrity validates it; no SAVE_VERSION bump. Discovery '발견' surfaced in WorldRevealModal + a collection-lite view (MenuPanels). TICK UNTOUCHED — advanceMonth diff empty; simulation.ts changed only for the derivationRules import + roguelite hydrate sanitizer. No-arg reset keeps standard run + empty collection (regression). Independently verified npm run harness:gate: 48 files / 493 tests.
features.next_step: Blocks #1-#2 landed (engine + cross-run discovery/collection). Next: block #3 — emergent effects (wire rule yields into conservative additive monthly bonuses via the same 2-line getMonthlyStrategicEffects hook; handoff reports/codex-handoff/v0_66_block3_archetype_effects.md, xhigh). Then #4 balance closes v0.66.

features.name: Final source art intake
features.description: Collect or generate final character, office object, and backdrop source PNGs after v0.56 blind-test P0 is closed.
definition_of_done: Character source art is provided as 1152x9600 RGBA PNG.
Office object source art is provided as 2560x1920 RGBA PNG.
Office backdrop source art is provided as 5120x2880 RGBA PNG.
assets:v053 and assets:v054 import the final sources without changing runtime frame contracts.
qa:office-visuals:screenshots is rerun and compared against the v0.55 baseline.
evidence: docs/ART_INTAKE.md and docs/ANTIGRAVITY_ART_BRIEF.md define source dimensions, import commands, and gate timing.
features.next_step: Start only after qa:asset-handoff reports final art request possible (gated by P2 AGY/real human blind playtest track).
